source.title: Staged docs for spending by award count

observations.statement: The endpoint is documented as POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_award_count/ on api.usaspending.gov and returns the number of awards in each award type for a filtered advanced-search result set.
The documented top-level request body includes required filters plus optional spending_level with values awards or subawards and optional subawards boolean.
Documentation says subawards defaults to false unless spending_level is set to subawards, in which case the default becomes true.

source.title: AdvancedFilterObject fields for spending by award count

observations.statement: The filters object documents many advanced-search fields including keywords, time_period, place of performance filters, agencies, recipient filters, award type and award id filters, amount and program filters, NAICS/TAS/PSC filters, pricing and competition filters, treasury account components, object class, program activity, and def_codes.
AgencyObject requires type, tier, and name, with optional toptier_name when tier is subtier.
ProgramActivityObject requires at least one of name or code when used.

Documentation states COVID-19 and IIJA def_codes interact with the subawards flag: prime-award filtering uses File C and non-zero COVID-related obligations or outlays, while subaward filtering requires sub_action_date on or after the disaster law enactment date.

source.title: Documented response structure for spending by award count

observations.statement: The documented response is a JSON object with results, spending_level, and optional messages.
For award-level responses, AwardTypeResult documents numeric counts for grants, loans, contracts, direct_payments, other, and idvs.

The same documentation file also defines SubawardTypeResult with numeric counts for subgrants and subcontracts, implying a different results shape when grouping at the subaward level even though the response section does not explicitly tie that object to the results field.

source.title: Route and test hits for spending_by_award_count

observations.statement: Repository search finds a routed view for ^spending_by_award_count and an integration test file dedicated to this endpoint, which increases confidence that the documented endpoint exists in source.

source.title: Current raw profile lookup for spending_by_award_count

observations.statement: No current raw profile content was loaded for this slug, so there is no existing promoted raw MCP request surface to compare against field-by-field.

source.title: Derived MCP coverage gap assessment
source.locator: Derived from staged docs plus missing current raw profile

observations.statement: Because no current raw profile is present, important request fields such as filters, spending_level, subawards, and high-value nested filters are effectively missing from the current MCP surface for this slug until a semantic bundle is promoted.
No pagination controls or page metadata are documented; this endpoint appears to return one aggregate object per request rather than a paginated result set.

source.title: Live probe for award-level count response

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.

observations.statement: A request with only filters.time_period succeeded with HTTP 200 and returned a JSON object containing results, spending_level, and messages.
When spending_level is omitted, the live response returned spending_level as awards, confirming the documented default behavior for the top-level mode.
The award-level results object contained numeric counts for contracts, direct_payments, grants, idvs, loans, and other.
The live response included a warning message that search time periods are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01 and recommends download endpoints for older data.

source.title: Live probe for subaward-level count response

response.bodyShape: object with subaward results, spending_level, messages

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.

observations.statement: A subaward-mode request with spending_level=subawards and subawards=true succeeded with HTTP 200.
The live subaward response returned spending_level as subawards and a results object containing numeric subgrants and subcontracts fields.
The same earliest-date warning message also appeared in subaward mode.

source.title: Reviewer report for disaster-code plus recipient-state award-count drilldown
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-1 for v2__search__spending_by_award_count

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that listRequestFields still labeled filters.def_codes, filters.recipient_scope, and filters.recipient_locations as documented_unverified before this repair.

The reviewer reported that validateRequest accepted the award-level request body {filters:{def_codes:["L"],recipient_scope:"domestic",recipient_locations:[{"country":"USA","state":"VA"}]}} but warned only because those three request paths were still marked documented_unverified.

The reviewer reported that callEndpoint with the same DEFC L plus Virginia recipient-location filter returned HTTP 200 and results {contracts:365, grants:47, direct_payments:0, idvs:0, loans:0, other:0}, confirming live support for that filter combination in award mode.

source.title: Reviewer report for disaster-geography state drilldown template
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2 for v2__search__spending_by_award_count

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that findWorkflows surfaced the promoted workflow "Map first, drill down second" on v2__disaster__spending_by_geography, which tells callers to keep the same DEFC filter and translate each non-null state shape_code into downstream award-search location filters.

The reviewer reported that the disaster-geography usage guidance maps state shape_code values into recipient_locations with recipient_scope=domestic when the upstream geography scope was recipient_location or its default, and into place_of_performance_locations with place_of_performance_scope=domestic when the upstream scope was place_of_performance.
The same guidance says not to translate the uncoded geography bucket where shape_code is null into a downstream state filter.

source.title: Reviewer report for disaster-geography state drilldown template
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2 for v2__search__spending_by_award_count

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that the synthesized downstream request body {filters:{def_codes:["L"],recipient_scope:"domestic",recipient_locations:[{"country":"USA","state":"VA"}]}} validated and returned HTTP 200 from POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_award_count/.
That reviewer-reported response showed the Virginia DEFC L slice was contract-heavy, with results {contracts:365, grants:47, direct_payments:0, idvs:0, loans:0, other:0}.

source.title: Reviewer report for award-count filters parent status
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-award-count-filters-parent-status for v2__search__spending_by_award_count

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that validateRequest accepted the award-level request body {filters:{def_codes:["L"],recipient_scope:"domestic",recipient_locations:[{"country":"USA","state":"VA"}]}} as valid but still warned that top-level filters was only documented/unverified.

The reviewer reported that callEndpoint with the same request returned HTTP 200 and results {contracts:365, grants:47, direct_payments:0, idvs:0, loans:0, other:0}, demonstrating live support for the parent filters object on this promoted disaster-geography recipient-state drilldown shape.

The reviewer reported that listRequestFields still labeled request.filters as documented_unverified even though the child paths request.filters.def_codes, request.filters.recipient_scope, and request.filters.recipient_locations were already documented_and_observed, identifying the stale parent-status mismatch this repair corrects.
